title: Earnings Call Transcript: Bloom Energy Surpasses Q4 2025 Earnings Forecasts
text: Bloom Energy's Q4 2025 earnings call transcript revealed management's perspective on full-year 2025 performance and outlook for 2026. The company surpassed Q4 2025 earnings forecasts with revenue of $777.7 million versus expected $640 million, representing 21.5% upside. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.45 beat forecast of $0.30 by 50%. Full-year 2025 revenue reached record $2.02 billion, up 37.3% from prior year. The company achieved significant margin expansion with gross margin reaching 31.9% in Q4. Adjusted EBITDA of $271.6 million demonstrated strong profitability achievement. Management noted that 2025 represented inflection year as AI data center demand accelerated dramatically. The $5 billion Brookfield partnership was characterized as transformational for enabling rapid global scaling. Oracle's partnership expansion was highlighted as validation of Bloom's technology as preferred power solution. Management provided 2026 guidance range of $3.1-$3.3 billion revenue with 32% non-GAAP gross margin. Operating income guidance of $425-$475 million reflected improved profitability. The backlog of approximately $20 billion provided multi-year revenue visibility. Management emphasized continued focus on execution and manufacturing scalability supporting backlog conversion.
